title: Technician Tool Assisted Task
description: Download an official technician tool, verify it, run it for a bounded task, verify the result, then remove the downloaded tool.
starter_prompt: Use an official technician tool to complete the requested task. Download it to session artifacts, verify it, run it only after approval, verify the result, then remove the downloaded tool.

phases.prompt: Confirm the task goal, official tool source, expected output, and any risk before download.

Review the supplied official HTTPS URL for ${tool_name}. Identify vendor, expected signature/checksum guidance, license/safety notes, and whether the tool fits this task goal: ${task_goal}. Do not execute anything.
phases.completion: Official source and safety notes are documented.

phases.prompt: Download the official technician tool into the session artifacts/downloads folder.
phases.completion: Downloaded tool path, size, SHA-256, and source URL are captured.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $tool='${result_download_tool_path}'; Write-Output '== Downloaded tool =='; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($tool) -or -not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $tool)) { throw 'Downloaded tool path missing' }; Get-Item -LiteralPath $tool | Select-Object FullName,Length,LastWriteTime | Format-List; Write-Output '== SHA256 =='; Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -LiteralPath $tool | Format-List; Write-Output 'Download phase SHA256: ${result_download_tool_sha256}'; Write-Output 'Source URL: ${result_download_tool_source_url}'

phases.prompt: Verify downloaded tool path, size, SHA-256, and source before execution.
phases.completion: Downloaded tool integrity evidence is captured.

phases.prompt: Ask human or apply access-mode policy before running the downloaded tool. Offer restore point when needed.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $tool='${result_download_tool_path}'; if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($tool) -or -not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $tool)) { throw 'Downloaded tool path missing' }; Write-Output '== Running downloaded technician tool =='; Write-Output ('Tool: ' + $tool); Write-Output 'Task goal: ${task_goal}'; & $tool ${tool_arguments}; $exit=$LASTEXITCODE; if ($null -eq $exit) { $exit=0 }; Write-Output ('tool-exit-code=' + $exit); exit $exit

phases.prompt: Run the downloaded tool with the supplied arguments only after approval. Capture stdout/stderr and exit code.
phases.completion: Tool run result and logs are captured.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Tool run verification =='; Write-Output 'Task goal: ${task_goal}'; Write-Output 'Tool exit code from run phase: ${result_run_tool_exit_code}'; $tool='${result_download_tool_path}'; if (Test-Path -LiteralPath $tool) { Get-Item -LiteralPath $tool | Select-Object FullName,Length,LastWriteTime | Format-List; Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -LiteralPath $tool | Format-List } else { Write-Output 'Downloaded tool already absent' }

phases.prompt: Verify the tool run result, exit code, and remaining artifact state.
Analyze source review, download verification, tool run output, exit code, and post-run verification. Decide whether the task goal was met and what evidence proves it.
phases.completion: Task completion confidence and evidence are documented.

phases.prompt: Create a technician handoff report with task goal, official source, downloaded artifact hash, command/tools used, actions taken, verification evidence, cleanup status, residual risk, artifacts, and follow-up.
phases.completion: User has a clear technician report.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; $tool='${result_download_tool_path}'; Write-Output '== Cleanup downloaded technician tool =='; if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($tool) -and (Test-Path -LiteralPath $tool)) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $tool -Force; Write-Output ('Removed downloaded tool: ' + $tool) } else { Write-Output 'No downloaded tool file to remove' }; Write-Output 'Logs, reports, hashes, and command output are preserved in session artifacts.'

phases.prompt: Remove the downloaded technician tool from artifacts after preserving logs, hash, source URL, and report evidence.
phases.completion: Downloaded tool is removed or cleanup debt is recorded.

acceptance_criteria: Tool source is HTTPS and official or explicitly rejected.
Downloaded file path, size, SHA-256, and source URL are captured before execution.
Tool execution is approved according to access mode and logs are preserved.
Post-run verification determines whether the task goal was met.
Downloaded tool is removed after completion while reports and logs remain.
